title: Anthropic taps Musk's Colossus as compute scarcity binds rivals - TCR 05/07/26
description:
Anthropic signed for more than 300 MW of compute at xAI's Colossus 1 supercomputer in Memphis pushing disclosed compute commitments past $330 billion, Anthropic co-founder Jack Clark published a 60% probability of recursive AI self-improvement before end of 2028 with Anthropic's internal training-code benchmark moving from 2.9x to 52x in under a year, and AEP CEO Bill Fehrman told analysts AEP is reviewing exit from PJM and SPP with 63 GW of contracted new large load and 190 GW in queue.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-may-7-2026/

0:00 Anthropic-SpaceX Colossus compute deal
4:19 When Rivals Share A Power Cord
10:20 Mira Murati testimony, RedAccess audit, persistence study
16:42 Moderna Phase 3 mRNA flu vaccine
18:58 Close

This episode covers the Anthropic-SpaceX Colossus deal and the entangled hyperscaler capital architecture, Jack Clark's 60% recursive self-improvement estimate and the METR and SWE-Bench curves behind it, AEP's threat to leave PJM and SPP over interconnection delays, Mira Murati's sworn testimony in Musk v. Altman about OpenAI's deployment safety board, the RedAccess audit of 5,000+ vibe-coded apps exposing medical and corporate data, the Bakker team's hints-versus-direct-answers design finding for educational AI, and Moderna's Phase 3 mRNA flu vaccine result published in the New England Journal of Medicine.

The Anthropic-SpaceX deal arrives because compute scarcity has become more powerful than the rivalries that were supposed to organize this industry. Anthropic's disclosed compute commitments now exceed $330 billion against $30-40 billion in revenue, and roughly half of more than $2 trillion in cloud backlog now traces back to two labs. Jack Clark walked through the curves: METR's autonomous task-completion horizon went from 30 seconds to 12 hours in four years, SWE-Bench from 2% to 93.9%, and Anthropic's training-code optimization benchmark from 2.9x to 52x in less than a year. The verification gap is sharpening on the same timeline - Murati's testimony enters the trial record as a recoverable artifact, and RedAccess produced an audited inventory of 5,000+ exposed apps that platform liability law can cite by name. Separately, Carnegie Mellon, MIT, Oxford, and UCLA handed educators a measurable design distinction for AI-integrated learning: AI configured to give hints preserves persistence; AI configured to give direct answers does not. Underneath, AEP signaled that the regional transmission organizations may no longer be the right venue to coordinate U.S. electricity markets, while Moderna's Phase 3 flu vaccine result confirms the mRNA platform works as a generalizable architecture compressing the 12-month strain-selection cycle that has constrained influenza vaccine design for half a century.

- NBC News - Moderna's mRNA flu vaccine more effective than standard shot: https://www.nbcnews.com/health/health-news/moderna-mrna-flu-vaccine-better-trial-fda-rcna343857
